chapter_title.ar: باب اسْتِيمَارِ الْبِكْرِ وَ مَنْ يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ اسْتِيمَارُهَا وَ مَنْ لَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – Permission of (marriage of) the virgin and the one Obligated upon permitting her, and the one on who there is no such Obligation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/130)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/130)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الْمَرْأَةِ الْبِكْرِ إِذْنُهَا صُمَاتُهَا وَ الثَّيِّبِ أَمْرُهَا إِلَيْهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, form Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr who said,
‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said regarding the virgin woman, her permission is her silence, and the divorced/widow, her command is in her (control)’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad and Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘In the case of a virgin, her remaining quiet is her permission, and a woman for her second marriage has her own command and power of decision- making.’”
